name: The Bronto-Rib Rack
description: A massive, bone-in rack of smoked beef short ribs slathered in a sticky molasses-BBQ glaze. It’s so heavy it’s guaranteed to tip your stone-wheeled car over when served.

name: Pterodactyl Wings
description: Giant jumbo turkey wings, dry-rubbed with wild herbs and wood-fired until the skin is perfectly crisp. Served with a side of spicy "lava" dipping sauce.

name: Saber-Tooth Tartare
description: Coarsely hand-chopped prime beef mixed with wild capers, sea salt, and a raw quail egg yolk on top. It's served over a flat, chilled river stone.

name: Yabba-Dabba Double Burger
description: Two thick, hand-pressed bison patties topped with melted smoked gouda, thick-cut wild boar bacon, and a fried egg, all sandwiched between two heavy, artisanal potato-bread "buns."

name: Bedrock Bone Marrow Canoe
description: A roasted, split beef femur overflowing with rich, buttery marrow. It’s topped with a coarse herb chimichurri and served with grilled flatbread for scooping.

name: The Gravelberry Tart
description: A rustic, free-form galette packed with wild blackberries, huckleberries, and elderberries, baked in a flaky, stone-ground wheat crust and drizzled with raw honey.

name: Mammoth Meatball Skewers
description: Enormous, fire-roasted pork and venison meatballs skewered on a sharpened rosemary branch, served over a bed of smoky roasted root vegetables.

name: Prehistoric Pasta Nest
description: Thick, hand-rolled ribbon pasta tossed in a rich, dark wild mushroom and truffle cream sauce, served inside a hollowed-out, roasted acorn squash "bowl."

name: The Dino-Drumstick
description: An oversized emu or goose leg, slow-braised in a deep red wine broth until the meat is incredibly tender, then flash-fried for a crackling exterior.

name: The Tar Pit Lava Cake
description: A rich, dark chocolate fondant cake with a molten fudge center that flows like lava when cut open, dusted with crushed "ash" (black sugar crystals).
